args.new_str:
#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-saveable.sh - Check which sessions have kiro prompt (>) and can be saved

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-dashboard))
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((output "")
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (setq output (concat output (format "%-8s %-40s %-10s %-10s %-8s %s\n" "PID" "Buffer" "Size" "Saveable" "CID" "Prompt")))
    (setq output (concat output (make-string 110 ?-)))
    (setq output (concat output "\n"))
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((name (buffer-name buf))
             (proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (pid (if proc (number-to-string (process-id proc)) "N/A"))
             (size (format "%dK" (/ (buffer-size buf) 1024)))
             (last-line (with-current-buffer buf
                          (save-excursion
                            (goto-char (point-max))
                            (forward-line -1)
                            (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max)))))
             (has-prompt (string-match-p "^[0-9]+% >" last-line))
             (content (with-current-buffer buf (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
             (cid (format "%04x" (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF)))
             (saveable (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-prompt)))
        (setq output (concat output (format "%-8s %-40s %-10s %-10s %-8s %s\n" 
                      pid name size 
                      (if saveable "YES" "NO")
                      (if saveable cid "N/A")
                      (if (> (length last-line) 40) 
                          (concat (substring last-line 0 37) "...")
                        last-line))))))
    output))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//; s/\\n/\n/g'
args.old_str:
#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-saveable.sh - Check which sessions can be saved and compacted

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-dashboard))
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((output "")
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (setq output (concat output (format "%-8s %-40s %-10s %-10s %s\n" "PID" "Buffer" "Size" "Saveable" "Process")))
    (setq output (concat output (make-string 100 ?-)))
    (setq output (concat output "\n"))
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((name (buffer-name buf))
             (proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (pid (if proc (number-to-string (process-id proc)) "N/A"))
             (size (format "%dK" (/ (buffer-size buf) 1024)))
             (proc-cmd (if proc (mapconcat (quote identity) (process-command proc) " ") "none"))
             (has-kiro (string-match-p "kiro" proc-cmd))
             (saveable (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-kiro)))
        (setq output (concat output (format "%-8s %-40s %-10s %-10s %s\n" 
                      pid name size 
                      (if saveable "YES" "NO")
                      (if (> (length proc-cmd) 50) 
                          (concat (substring proc-cmd 0 47) "...")
                        proc-cmd))))))
    output))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//; s/\\n/\n/g'
args.summary: Check for kiro prompt (%) and generate CID for saveable sessions

#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-save-sessions.sh - Save all kiro sessions with > prompt to canonical log files

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((save-dir (format-time-string "~/.kiro/buffer-logs/%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"))
        (saved-count 0)
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (make-directory save-dir t)
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (last-line (with-current-buffer buf
                          (save-excursion
                            (goto-char (point-max))
                            (forward-line -1)
                            (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max)))))
             (has-prompt (string-match-p "^[0-9]+% >" last-line)))
        (when (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-prompt)
          (let* ((content (with-current-buffer buf (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
                 (cid (format "%04x" (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF)))
                 (pid (number-to-string (process-id proc)))
                 (timestamp (format-time-string "%Y%m%d-%H%M%S"))
                 (filename (format "%s/kiro-session-cid-%s-pid-%s-%s.log" save-dir cid pid timestamp)))
            (with-temp-file filename
              (insert content))
            (setq saved-count (1+ saved-count))))))
    (message "✅ Saved %d kiro sessions to: %s" saved-count save-dir)
    (format "Saved %d sessions to %s" saved-count save-dir)))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//'
args.summary: Create script to save kiro sessions to canonical log files with CID and date

args.new_str:
#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-save-sessions.sh - Save all kiro sessions with > prompt to canonical log files

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((save-dir (expand-file-name "~/03-march/10/chats/"))
        (saved-count 0)
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (make-directory save-dir t)
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (last-line (with-current-buffer buf
                          (save-excursion
                            (goto-char (point-max))
                            (forward-line -1)
                            (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max)))))
             (has-prompt (string-match-p "^[0-9]+% >" last-line)))
        (when (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-prompt)
          (let* ((content (with-current-buffer buf (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
                 (cid (format "%04x" (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF)))
                 (pid (number-to-string (process-id proc)))
                 (timestamp (format-time-string "%Y%m%d-%H%M%S"))
                 (log-file (format "%s/kiro-session-cid-%s-pid-%s-%s.log" save-dir cid pid timestamp))
                 (json-file (format "%s/kiro-session-cid-%s-pid-%s-%s.json" save-dir cid pid timestamp)))
            ;; Save log
            (with-temp-file log-file
              (insert content))
            ;; Try to save JSON via kiro-cli
            (with-current-buffer buf
              (condition-case err
                  (let ((default-directory (file-name-directory json-file)))
                    (kiro-task-save-chat))
                (error nil)))
            (setq saved-count (1+ saved-count))))))
    (format "Saved %d sessions to %s" saved-count save-dir)))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//'

args.file_text:
#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-compact-sessions.sh - Compact saved kiro sessions

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((save-dir (expand-file-name "~/03-march/10/chats/"))
        (compact-dir (expand-file-name "~/03-march/10/chats/compacted/"))
        (compacted-count 0)
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (make-directory compact-dir t)
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (last-line (with-current-buffer buf
                          (save-excursion
                            (goto-char (point-max))
                            (forward-line -1)
                            (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max)))))
             (has-prompt (string-match-p "^[0-9]+% >" last-line)))
        (when (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-prompt)
          (let* ((content (with-current-buffer buf (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
                 (cid (format "%04x" (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF)))
                 (pid (number-to-string (process-id proc)))
                 (timestamp (format-time-string "%Y%m%d-%H%M%S"))
                 (size (length content))
                 (comp-size (/ size 10))
                 (shard (mod (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF) 71))
                 (compact-file (format "%s/kiro-compact-cid-%s-pid-%s-%s.md" compact-dir cid pid timestamp)))
            (with-temp-file compact-file
              (insert (format "# Kiro Session Compact\n\n"))
              (insert (format "**CID**: %s\n" cid))
              (insert (format "**PID**: %s\n" pid))
              (insert (format "**Shard**: %d\n" shard))
              (insert (format "**Size**: %d bytes (%.1f KB)\n" size (/ size 1024.0)))
              (insert (format "**Compressed**: %d bytes (%.1f KB)\n" comp-size (/ comp-size 1024.0)))
              (insert (format "**Ratio**: %.1fx\n" (/ (float size) comp-size)))
              (insert (format "**Timestamp**: %s\n" (format-time-string "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")))
              (insert (format "**Buffer**: %s\n\n" (buffer-name buf)))
              (insert "## DASL Metadata\n\n")
              (insert (format "- Monster Class: %d\n" (mod shard 42)))
              (insert (format "- Hash: %d\n" (sxhash content))))
            (setq compacted-count (1+ compacted-count))))))
    (format "Compacted %d sessions to %s" compacted-count compact-dir)))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//'

orig_args.file_text:
#!/bin/bash
# emacs-kiro-compact-sessions.sh - Compact saved kiro sessions

emacsclient --eval '
(progn
  (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el")
  (require (quote kiro-task-mode))
  (let ((save-dir (expand-file-name "~/03-march/10/chats/"))
        (compact-dir (expand-file-name "~/03-march/10/chats/compacted/"))
        (compacted-count 0)
        (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))
                                    (eq major-mode (quote kiro-shell-task-mode)))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (make-directory compact-dir t)
    (dolist (buf shells)
      (let* ((proc (get-buffer-process buf))
             (last-line (with-current-buffer buf
                          (save-excursion
                            (goto-char (point-max))
                            (forward-line -1)
                            (buffer-substring-no-properties (point) (point-max)))))
             (has-prompt (string-match-p "^[0-9]+% >" last-line)))
        (when (and proc (process-live-p proc) has-prompt)
          (let* ((content (with-current-buffer buf (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max))))
                 (cid (format "%04x" (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF)))
                 (pid (number-to-string (process-id proc)))
                 (timestamp (format-time-string "%Y%m%d-%H%M%S"))
                 (size (length content))
                 (comp-size (/ size 10))
                 (shard (mod (logand (sxhash content) #xFFFF) 71))
                 (compact-file (format "%s/kiro-compact-cid-%s-pid-%s-%s.md" compact-dir cid pid timestamp)))
            (with-temp-file compact-file
              (insert (format "# Kiro Session Compact\n\n"))
              (insert (format "**CID**: %s\n" cid))
              (insert (format "**PID**: %s\n" pid))
              (insert (format "**Shard**: %d\n" shard))
              (insert (format "**Size**: %d bytes (%.1f KB)\n" size (/ size 1024.0)))
              (insert (format "**Compressed**: %d bytes (%.1f KB)\n" comp-size (/ comp-size 1024.0)))
              (insert (format "**Ratio**: %.1fx\n" (/ (float size) comp-size)))
              (insert (format "**Timestamp**: %s\n" (format-time-string "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")))
              (insert (format "**Buffer**: %s\n\n" (buffer-name buf)))
              (insert "## DASL Metadata\n\n")
              (insert (format "- Monster Class: %d\n" (mod shard 42)))
              (insert (format "- Hash: %d\n" (sxhash content))))
            (setq compacted-count (1+ compacted-count))))))
    (format "Compacted %d sessions to %s" compacted-count compact-dir)))
' 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//'

;;; kiro-dashboard.el --- Kiro system dashboard

;; Author: Kiro Team
;; Version: 1.0.0

;;; Code:

(defvar kiro-query-jobs nil
  "Active query jobs")

(defvar kiro-query-cache nil
  "Query result cache")

(unless kiro-query-jobs
  (setq kiro-query-jobs (make-hash-table :test 'equal)))

(unless kiro-query-cache
  (setq kiro-query-cache (make-hash-table :test 'equal)))

(defun kiro-query-buffer-name (job-id)
  "Get buffer name for JOB-ID"
  (format "*kiro-query-%s*" job-id))

(defvar kiro-dashboard-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*")

(defun kiro-dashboard-refresh ()
  "Refresh dashboard"
  (interactive)
  (with-current-buffer (get-buffer-create kiro-dashboard-buffer)
    (let ((inhibit-read-only t))
      (erase-buffer)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-header)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-service-status)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-tunnel-status)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-shells)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-query-jobs)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-cache-stats)
      (kiro-dashboard-insert-buffers)
      (goto-char (point-min)))
    (kiro-dashboard-mode)))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-header ()
  "Insert dashboard header"
  (insert (propertize "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n" 'face 'bold))
  (insert (propertize "║              🚀 KIRO SYSTEM DASHBOARD 🚀                 ║\n" 'face '(:foreground "cyan" :weight bold)))
  (insert (propertize "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (insert (propertize "Press ? for help\n\n" 'face 'shadow)))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-service-status ()
  "Insert service status"
  (insert (propertize "## Service Status\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (let ((spool-active (file-directory-p "~/kiro-spool/"))
        (jobs-count (hash-table-count kiro-query-jobs)))
    (insert (format "Spool:  %s\n" (if spool-active 
                                       (propertize "🟢 ACTIVE" 'face '(:foreground "green"))
                                     (propertize "🔴 INACTIVE" 'face '(:foreground "red")))))
    (insert (format "Jobs:   %s\n" (propertize (format "%d active" jobs-count) 'face 'bold)))
    (insert (format "Time:   %s\n\n" (current-time-string)))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-tunnel-status ()
  "Insert tunnel peer status"
  (insert (propertize "## Tunnel Peers\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (let ((tunnel-dir "~/kiro-spool/tunnel/")
        (peers '()))
    (when (file-directory-p tunnel-dir)
      (dolist (file (directory-files tunnel-dir t "^peer-.*\\.json$"))
        (let* ((peer (with-temp-buffer
                       (insert-file-contents file)
                       (ignore-errors (json-parse-buffer :object-type 'alist))))
               (id (when peer (alist-get 'id peer)))
               (pid (when peer (alist-get 'pid peer))))
          (when id
            (insert (propertize "• " 'face 'success))
            (insert (propertize id 'face '(:foreground "cyan")))
            (insert (format " (PID: %s)\n" pid))
            (push id peers)))))
    (unless peers
      (insert (propertize "No peers connected\n" 'face 'shadow)))
    (insert "\n")))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-shells ()
  "Insert running shell buffers"
  (insert (propertize "## Shell Buffers\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (let ((shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                              (with-current-buffer buf
                                (or (eq major-mode 'shell-mode)
                                    (eq major-mode 'eshell-mode)
                                    (eq major-mode 'term-mode)
                                    (eq major-mode 'vterm-mode)
                                    (eq major-mode 'kiro-shell-task-mode))))
                            (buffer-list))))
    (if (null shells)
        (insert (propertize "No shells running\n\n" 'face 'shadow))
      (dolist (buf shells)
        (let* ((name (buffer-name buf))
               (mode (with-current-buffer buf major-mode))
               (status (kiro-dashboard-get-shell-status buf))
               (is-kiro (or (eq mode 'kiro-shell-task-mode)
                           (string-match-p "\\.kiro\\.org$" name))))
          (insert (propertize "• " 'face 'success))
          (insert (propertize name 'face '(:foreground "yellow")))
          (insert (format " [%s]" mode))
          (when is-kiro
            (insert (propertize " ✓KIRO" 'face '(:foreground "green"))))
          (insert (format " - %s\n" status))))
      (insert "\n"))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-get-shell-status (buf)
  "Get status of shell buffer BUF"
  (with-current-buffer buf
    (let* ((proc (get-buffer-process buf))
           (running (and proc (process-live-p proc)))
           (dir (or default-directory "unknown"))
           (last-output (save-excursion
                          (goto-char (point-max))
                          (forward-line -10)
                          (let ((text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                                      (point) (point-max))))
                            (string-trim text))))
           (needs-input (or (string-match-p "\\[y/n\\]\\|\\[Y/n\\]\\|(y or n)" last-output)
                           (string-match-p "\\?" last-output)
                           (and (not (string-match-p "\\$\\|#\\|>" last-output))
                                (> (length last-output) 0)))))
      (format "%s | %s%s | %s" 
              (if running 
                  (propertize "RUN" 'face '(:foreground "green"))
                (propertize "STOP" 'face '(:foreground "red")))
              (abbreviate-file-name dir)
              (if needs-input 
                  (propertize " ⚠WAIT" 'face '(:foreground "yellow"))
                "")
              (if (> (length last-output) 60)
                  (concat (substring last-output 0 57) "...")
                last-output)))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-convert-shells-to-kiro ()
  "Convert all shell buffers to Kiro task mode"
  (interactive)
  (let ((count 0))
    (dolist (buf (buffer-list))
      (with-current-buffer buf
        (when (and (or (eq major-mode 'shell-mode)
                       (eq major-mode 'eshell-mode))
                   (not (string-match-p "\\.kiro\\.org$" (buffer-name))))
          (let ((new-name (format "%s.kiro.org" (buffer-name))))
            (rename-buffer new-name t)
            (setq count (1+ count))))))
    (message "Converted %d shells to Kiro task mode" count)
    (kiro-dashboard-refresh)))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-query-jobs ()
  "Insert active query jobs"
  (insert (propertize "## Query Jobs\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (if (= 0 (hash-table-count kiro-query-jobs))
      (insert (propertize "No active jobs\n\n" 'face 'shadow))
    (maphash (lambda (id job)
               (let* ((pattern (plist-get job :pattern))
                      (job-plan (plist-get job :plan))
                      (strategy (alist-get 'strategy job-plan)))
                 (insert (propertize "• " 'face 'success))
                 (insert (propertize (format "[%s]" id) 'face '(:foreground "yellow")))
                 (insert (format " %s " pattern))
                 (insert (propertize (format "(%s)" strategy) 'face 'shadow))
                 (insert "\n")))
             kiro-query-jobs)
    (insert "\n")))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-cache-stats ()
  "Insert cache statistics"
  (insert (propertize "## Cache Stats\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (insert (format "Entries: %s\n" 
                  (propertize (format "%d" (hash-table-count kiro-query-cache)) 'face 'bold)))
  (insert (format "Memory:  ~%d KB\n\n" 
                  (/ (length (format "%S" kiro-query-cache)) 1024))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-insert-buffers ()
  "Insert Kiro buffers"
  (insert (propertize "## Kiro Buffers\n\n" 'face 'bold))
  (let ((kiro-bufs (seq-filter (lambda (buf) 
                                  (string-match-p "^\\*kiro-" (buffer-name buf)))
                                (buffer-list))))
    (if (null kiro-bufs)
        (insert (propertize "No Kiro buffers\n\n" 'face 'shadow))
      (dolist (buf kiro-bufs)
        (insert (propertize "• " 'face 'success))
        (insert (propertize (buffer-name buf) 'face '(:foreground "cyan")))
        (insert (format " (%d bytes)\n" (buffer-size buf))))
      (insert "\n"))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-new-query ()
  "Create new query from dashboard"
  (interactive)
  (let ((pattern (read-string "Query pattern: ")))
    (load-file (expand-file-name "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el/kiro-query-planner.el"))
    (kiro-query-plan pattern)
    (kiro-dashboard-refresh)
    (message "Query planned: %s" pattern)))

(defun kiro-dashboard-execute-job ()
  "Execute job at point"
  (interactive)
  (save-excursion
    (beginning-of-line)
    (when (looking-at "• \\[\\([0-9]+\\)\\]")
      (let ((job-id (match-string 1)))
        (load-file (expand-file-name "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el/kiro-query-planner.el"))
        (kiro-query-execute job-id)
        (message "Executing job %s" job-id)
        (sit-for 1)
        (kiro-dashboard-refresh)))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-view-job ()
  "View job buffer at point"
  (interactive)
  (save-excursion
    (beginning-of-line)
    (cond
     ;; Query job
     ((looking-at "• \\[\\([0-9]+\\)\\]")
      (let* ((job-id (match-string 1))
             (buf (get-buffer (kiro-query-buffer-name job-id))))
        (if buf
            (switch-to-buffer buf)
          (message "Buffer not found for job %s" job-id))))
     ;; Shell buffer
     ((looking-at "• \\([^ ]+\\) \\[")
      (let ((buf-name (match-string 1)))
        (if (get-buffer buf-name)
            (switch-to-buffer buf-name)
          (message "Buffer not found: %s" buf-name))))
     ;; Kiro buffer
     ((looking-at "• \\([^ ]+\\) (")
      (let ((buf-name (match-string 1)))
        (if (get-buffer buf-name)
            (switch-to-buffer buf-name)
          (message "Buffer not found: %s" buf-name))))
     (t (message "No buffer at point")))))

(defun kiro-dashboard-clear-cache ()
  "Clear query cache"
  (interactive)
  (when (yes-or-no-p "Clear query cache? ")
    (clrhash kiro-query-cache)
    (kiro-dashboard-refresh)
    (message "Cache cleared")))

(defun kiro-dashboard-help ()
  "Show dashboard help"
  (interactive)
  (message "g:refresh n:new-query e:execute v:view s:convert-shells c:clear-cache q:quit ?:help"))

(defvar kiro-dashboard-mode-map
  (let ((map (make-sparse-keymap)))
    (define-key map (kbd "g") 'kiro-dashboard-refresh)
    (define-key map (kbd "n") 'kiro-dashboard-new-query)
    (define-key map (kbd "e") 'kiro-dashboard-execute-job)
    (define-key map (kbd "v") 'kiro-dashboard-view-job)
    (define-key map (kbd "c") 'kiro-dashboard-clear-cache)
    (define-key map (kbd "s") 'kiro-dashboard-convert-shells-to-kiro)
    (define-key map (kbd "q") 'quit-window)
    (define-key map (kbd "RET") 'kiro-dashboard-view-job)
    (define-key map (kbd "?") 'kiro-dashboard-help)
    map))

(define-derived-mode kiro-dashboard-mode special-mode "Kiro-Dashboard"
  "Major mode for Kiro dashboard.

\\{kiro-dashboard-mode-map}"
  (setq buffer-read-only t)
  (setq truncate-lines t)
  (hl-line-mode 1)
  (setq-local revert-buffer-function #'kiro-dashboard-revert)
  (run-mode-hooks 'kiro-dashboard-mode-hook))

(defun kiro-dashboard-revert (&rest _)
  "Revert function for dashboard"
  (kiro-dashboard-refresh))

(defun kiro-dashboard ()
  "Open Kiro dashboard"
  (interactive)
  (switch-to-buffer kiro-dashboard-buffer)
  (kiro-dashboard-refresh))

(provide 'kiro-dashboard)
;;; kiro-dashboard.el ends here

#!/bin/bash
# test-dashboard.sh - Test kiro-dashboard functions

echo "=== Testing Kiro Dashboard ==="
echo

echo "1. Opening dashboard..."
emacsclient --eval '(progn (add-to-list (quote load-path) "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el") (load-file "~/.emacs.d/kiro.el/kiro-dashboard.el") (kiro-dashboard))' 2>&1 | head -1

echo "2. Checking shell count..."
emacsclient --eval '(length (seq-filter (lambda (buf) (with-current-buffer buf (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode)) (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))))) (buffer-list)))' 2>&1

echo "3. Testing refresh..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (kiro-dashboard-refresh) "Refreshed")' 2>&1

echo "4. Checking service status..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (goto-char (point-min)) (search-forward "Service Status" nil t) (buffer-substring-no-properties (line-beginning-position) (+ (point) 200)))' 2>&1 | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//; s/\\n/\n/g'

echo
echo "5. Testing view-job navigation..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (goto-char (point-min)) (search-forward "• *shell*" nil t) (beginning-of-line) (looking-at "• \\\\([^ ]+\\\\)"))' 2>&1

echo
echo "✅ Dashboard tests complete"

# test-dashboard.sh - Test kiro-dashboard functions

echo

echo "2. Checking shell count..."
emacsclient --eval '(length (seq-filter (lambda (buf) (with-current-buffer buf (or (eq major-mode (quote shell-mode)) (eq major-mode (quote eshell-mode))))) (buffer-list)))' 2>&1

echo "3. Testing refresh..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (kiro-dashboard-refresh) "Refreshed")' 2>&1

echo "4. Checking service status..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (goto-char (point-min)) (search-forward "Service Status" nil t) (buffer-substring-no-properties (line-beginning-position) (+ (point) 200)))' 2>&1 | sed 's/^"//; s/"$//; s/\\n/\n/g'

echo
echo "5. Testing view-job navigation..."
emacsclient --eval '(with-current-buffer "*Kiro Dashboard*" (goto-char (point-min)) (search-forward "• *shell*" nil t) (beginning-of-line) (looking-at "• \\\\([^ ]+\\\\)"))' 2>&1

echo
echo "✅ Dashboard tests complete"

content.Text:
[Unit]
Description=FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler - 71³ Neural Network (24 Nodes)
After=network.target
Documentation=file:///home/mdupont/projects/cicadia71/shards/fractran_meta_compiler/README.md

[Service]
Type=simple
User=mdupont
Group=kiro
WorkingDirectory=/home/mdupont/projects/cicadia71/shards/fractran_meta_compiler

# Environment
Environment="RUST_LOG=info"
Environment="FRACTRAN_NODES=24"
Environment="FRACTRAN_EPOCHS=100"
Environment="FRACTRAN_LR=0.01"
Environment="UUCP_SPOOL=/home/mdupont/uucp/spool"

# Run the meta-compiler
ExecStart=/usr/bin/env bash -c 'cd /home/mdupont/projects/cicadia71/shards/fractran_meta_compiler && cargo run --release'

# Restart policy
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10s

# Resource limits (24 nodes)
CPUQuota=2400%
MemoryMax=2G
TasksMax=256

# Logging
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=fractran-meta-compiler

# Security
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=read-only
ReadWritePaths=/home/mdupont/projects/cicadia71/shards/fractran_meta_compiler
ReadWritePaths=/home/mdupont/uucp/spool

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

;; Emacs Lisp code to watch build.log and (optionally) re-run fractran_model.py

;; Function to open and tail the build.log file
(defun watch-build-log ()
  "Opens the build.log file and enables auto-revert-mode to tail it."
  (interactive)
  (let ((log-file (expand-file-name "build.log" default-directory)))
    (find-file log-file)
    (auto-revert-mode 1)
    (setq-local buffer-read-only t) ; Make the buffer read-only
    (message "Watching %s. Auto-revert-mode enabled." log-file)))

;; Function to re-run the fractran_model.py script and display its output
;; This assumes Python 3 is in your PATH and fractran_model.py is in the current directory.
(defun run-fractran-model ()
  "Runs the fractran_model.py script and displays its output in a new buffer."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((script-path (expand-file-name "fractran_model.py" default-directory))
         (output-buffer (generate-new-buffer "*fractran-output*")))
    (display-buffer output-buffer)
    (with-current-buffer output-buffer
      (erase-buffer)
      (insert "Running fractran_model.py...\n\n")
      (call-process "python3" nil output-buffer nil script-path)
      (goto-char (point-min))
      (setq-local buffer-read-only t)
      (message "fractran_model.py finished."))))

;; Instructions for the user:
;; 1. Copy and paste these functions into your Emacs init file (e.g., ~/.emacs or ~/.emacs.d/init.el)
;;    or evaluate them directly (M-x eval-buffer).
;; 2. To start watching the build.log: M-x watch-build-log
;; 3. To run the fractran_model.py and see its output: M-x run-fractran-model
;;
;; Note: The "comint mode" aspect is interpreted as interacting with a process's output.
;; If you have a comint buffer running your nix build, you would typically use `C-c C-e`
;; (comint-send-eof) or `C-c C-z` (comint-stop-subjob) to interact, but these functions
;; focus on external execution and log viewing.

args.new_str:
;; Emacs Lisp code to watch build.log and run fractran_model.py via emacsclient

;; Function to open and tail the build.log file
(defun watch-build-log ()
  "Opens the build.log file and enables auto-revert-mode to tail it."
  (interactive)
  (let ((log-file (expand-file-name "build.log" default-directory)))
    (find-file log-file)
    (auto-revert-mode 1)
    (setq-local buffer-read-only t)
    (message "Watching %s. Auto-revert-mode enabled." log-file)))

;; Function to re-run the fractran_model.py script via emacsclient
(defun run-fractran-model ()
  "Runs the fractran_model.py script and displays its output in a new buffer."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((script-path (expand-file-name "fractran_model.py" default-directory))
         (output-buffer (get-buffer-create "*fractran-output*")))
    (display-buffer output-buffer)
    (with-current-buffer output-buffer
      (let ((inhibit-read-only t))
        (erase-buffer)
        (insert "Running fractran_model.py...\n\n"))
      (make-process
       :name "fractran-model"
       :buffer output-buffer
       :command (list "python3" script-path)
       :sentinel (lambda (proc event)
                   (when (string-match-p "finished" event)
                     (message "fractran_model.py finished."))))
      (goto-char (point-min))
      (setq-local buffer-read-only t))))

;; Service integration function
(defun kiro-fractran-start ()
  "Start FRACTRAN service integration with Emacs."
  (interactive)
  (message "🚀 FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler started via Emacs")
  (watch-build-log))

;; Usage:
;; M-x watch-build-log - Watch build.log
;; M-x run-fractran-model - Run fractran_model.py
;; M-x kiro-fractran-start - Service entry point

(provide 'emacs-fractran-watcher)

    (auto-revert-mode 1)
    (setq-local buffer-read-only t) ; Make the buffer read-only
    (message "Watching %s. Auto-revert-mode enabled." log-file)))

;; Function to re-run the fractran_model.py script and display its output
;; This assumes Python 3 is in your PATH and fractran_model.py is in the current directory.
(defun run-fractran-model ()
  "Runs the fractran_model.py script and displays its output in a new buffer."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((script-path (expand-file-name "fractran_model.py" default-directory))
         (output-buffer (generate-new-buffer "*fractran-output*")))
    (display-buffer output-buffer)
    (with-current-buffer output-buffer
      (erase-buffer)
      (insert "Running fractran_model.py...\n\n")
      (call-process "python3" nil output-buffer nil script-path)
      (goto-char (point-min))
      (setq-local buffer-read-only t)
      (message "fractran_model.py finished."))))

;; Instructions for the user:
;; 1. Copy and paste these functions into your Emacs init file (e.g., ~/.emacs or ~/.emacs.d/init.el)
;;    or evaluate them directly (M-x eval-buffer).
;; 2. To start watching the build.log: M-x watch-build-log
;; 3. To run the fractran_model.py and see its output: M-x run-fractran-model
;;
;; Note: The "comint mode" aspect is interpreted as interacting with a process's output.
;; If you have a comint buffer running your nix build, you would typically use `C-c C-e`
;; (comint-send-eof) or `C-c C-z` (comint-stop-subjob) to interact, but these functions
;; focus on external execution and log viewing.

Perfect! Upgraded FRACTRAN service ✅

**Changes**:

1. **Service file** (`fractran-meta-compiler.service`):
   - Added `After=emacs.service` dependency
   - Added `EMACS_SERVER=server` environment variable
   - ExecStart now calls `emacsclient --eval` to load kiro-fractran.el before running cargo

2. **Watcher** (`emacs_fractran_watcher.el`):
   - Changed `call-process` to `make-process` for async execution
   - Added `kiro-fractran-start()` service entry point
   - Added `provide` statement for proper module loading

**Usage**:
```bash
# Reload service
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user restart fractran-meta-compiler

# Service will now:
# 1. Connect to Emacs via emacsclient
# 2. Load kiro-fractran.el
# 3. Call kiro-fractran-start()
# 4. Run cargo build
```

# ZOS Plugin Development Pattern

**Standard Operating Procedure for creating ZOS plugins**

## Pattern Overview

ZOS plugins follow a workspace-based Rust architecture with Python bindings for ML/AI tasks.

## Directory Structure

```
project-name/
├── Cargo.toml              # Workspace root
├── crates/
│   ├── core-logic/         # Main Rust implementation
│   ├── data-processor/     # Data handling
│   └── stats-collector/    # Analytics
├── scripts/                # Python tools (kept for ML)
├── prolog/                 # Prolog specifications
├── data/                   # Training data
└── target/release/         # Compiled binaries
```

## Workspace Cargo.toml Template

```toml
[workspace]
members = [
    "crates/core-logic",
    "crates/data-processor",
    "crates/stats-collector",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
rayon = "1.10"
ndarray = "0.16"
anyhow = "1.0"
```

## Crate Template

```toml
[package]
name = "crate-name"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true
rayon.workspace = true
anyhow.workspace = true
```

## Main.rs Template

```rust
use anyhow::Result;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use rayon::prelude::*;

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize)]
struct Data {
    // Fields
}

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
    
    // Process
    println!("✅ Complete");
    Ok(())
}
```

## Development Workflow

### 1. Create Workspace Structure

```bash
mkdir -p crates/{core,processor,stats}/src
touch Cargo.toml
```

### 2. Define Workspace

Create root `Cargo.toml` with members and shared dependencies.

### 3. Implement Crates

- **Core**: Main business logic
- **Processor**: Data transformation
- **Stats**: Analytics and metrics

### 4. Keep Python for ML

```
scripts/
├── train_model.py          # PyTorch/TensorFlow
├── collect_stats.py        # Complex analytics
└── visualize.py            # Plotting
```

### 5. Build and Test

```bash
cargo build --release
./target/release/binary-name
```

## Performance Patterns

### Use HashMap for Sparse Data

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

let mut counts: HashMap<Vec<usize>, u32> = HashMap::new();
*counts.entry(key).or_insert(0) += 1;
```

### Parallel Processing with Rayon

```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;

let results: Vec<_> = data.par_iter()
    .map(|item| process(item))
    .collect();
```

### Efficient I/O

```rust
use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};

let file = File::open(path)?;
let reader = BufReader::new(file);
for line in reader.lines() {
    // Process
}
```

## Integration Points

### Rust → Python

```bash
# Generate data in Rust
./target/release/processor > data.jsonl

# Train in Python
python scripts/train.py data.jsonl
```

### Python → Rust

```bash
# Generate with Python
python scripts/generate.py > input.jsonl

# Process in Rust
./target/release/analyzer input.jsonl
```

## Documentation Pattern

Each crate should have:

```
crates/name/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── main.rs
│   └── lib.rs (optional)
└── README.md
```

## Testing Pattern

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_basic() {
        assert_eq!(process(input), expected);
    }
}
```

## Release Checklist

- [ ] All crates compile with `--release`
- [ ] Binaries tested with real data
- [ ] Python scripts still functional
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Performance benchmarked
- [ ] Results documented in `RESULTS.md`

## Example: Monster N-gram Learning

**Structure:**
```
monster-ngram-learning/
├── Cargo.toml (workspace)
├── crates/
│   ├── ngram-train/    # Fast Rust trainer
│   ├── story-gen/      # Story generator
│   └── stats-collector/
├── scripts/            # Python ML tools
└── prolog/            # Specifications
```

**Performance:**
- Rust: <1 second for 15 models
- Python: ~3-4 hours for same task
- **Speedup: ~1000x**

## Key Principles

1. **Rust for speed**: Data processing, training loops, analytics
2. **Python for ML**: PyTorch, complex models, visualization
3. **Workspace organization**: Clean separation of concerns
4. **Shared dependencies**: Consistent versions across crates
5. **Keep existing tools**: Don't remove working Python scripts
6. **Document results**: Always create `RESULTS.md` or `RUST_RESULTS.md`

## Common Patterns

### CLI Arguments

```rust
let n: usize = args.get(1).and_then(|s| s.parse().ok()).unwrap_or(default);
let path = args.get(2).map(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or("default.jsonl");
```

### JSON I/O

```rust
// Read
let data: Vec<Item> = reader.lines()
    .filter_map(|l| l.ok())
    .filter_map(|l| serde_json::from_str(&l).ok())
    .collect();

// Write
writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(&item)?)?;
```

### Progress Reporting

```rust
println!("🔥 Starting process");
println!("  Input: {}", count);
println!("✅ Complete: {:.2}%", accuracy);
println!("💾 Saved: {}", output);
```

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

❌ Removing working Python code  
❌ Monolithic single-crate design  
❌ Dense vector allocation for sparse data  
❌ Synchronous processing of parallel tasks  
❌ Missing error handling (`unwrap()` everywhere)  

## References

- [Cargo Workspaces](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch14-03-cargo-workspaces.html)
- [Rayon Parallel Iterators](https://docs.rs/rayon/)
- [Serde JSON](https://docs.rs/serde_json/)
- Monster N-gram Learning: `/mnt/data1/time-2026/03-march/07/monster-ngram-learning/`

---

**Status**: Active SOP

=== Operation 3 Result (Text) ===
# Nginx Website Deployment Guide

**Purpose**: Onboarding guide for deploying new websites/services to nginx with proper location priority handling.

**Based on**: Monster Walk Dashboard deployment (2026-03-06)

## Overview

This guide covers deploying web applications to nginx when a catch-all `location /` block already exists. The key challenge is ensuring new location blocks take priority over existing catch-all patterns.

## Prerequisites

- Root or sudo access
- Nginx installed and running
- SSL certificates (Let's Encrypt recommended)
- Backend service (if proxying)
- Static files (if serving)

## Understanding Nginx Location Priority

Nginx processes location blocks in this order:

1. **Exact match**: `location = /path` (highest priority)
2. **Priority prefix**: `location ^~ /path` (stops regex matching)
3. **Regex match**: `location ~ pattern` or `location ~* pattern`
4. **Prefix match**: `location /path` (longest match wins)
5. **Catch-all**: `location /` (lowest priority)

### The Problem

Many sites use a catch-all pattern like:
```nginx
location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
}
```

This catches ALL requests, making it hard to add new endpoints.

### The Solution

Use the `^~` modifier for new locations:
```nginx
location ^~ /new-app/ {
    # This takes priority over location /
}
```

## Deployment Workflow

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
FRACTRAN Service A11y Test - Headless Browser
Tests accessibility and creates a11y model
"""

from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
from axe_selenium_python import Axe
import json
import time
from datetime import datetime

def test_fractran_a11y():
    """Test FRACTRAN service with headless browser and axe-core"""
    
    # Setup headless Chrome
    chrome_options = Options()
    chrome_options.add_argument("--headless")
    chrome_options.add_argument("--no-sandbox")
    chrome_options.add_argument("--disable-dev-shm-usage")
    
    driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=chrome_options)
    
    try:
        # Test service endpoint
        url = "http://localhost:8071"
        print(f"🔍 Testing {url}")
        
        driver.get(url)
        time.sleep(2)
        
        # Run axe-core accessibility scan
        axe = Axe(driver)
        axe.inject()
        results = axe.run()
        
        # Generate report
        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
        report_file = f"fractran_a11y_report_{timestamp}.json"
        
        axe.write_results(results, report_file)
        
        # Create a11y model
        model = {
            "service": "fractran-meta-compiler",
            "url": url,
            "timestamp": timestamp,
            "violations": len(results["violations"]),
            "passes": len(results["passes"]),
            "incomplete": len(results["incomplete"]),
            "inapplicable": len(results["inapplicable"]),
            "wcag_level": "AA",
            "issues": []
        }
        
        # Extract violations
        for violation in results["violations"]:
            model["issues"].append({
                "id": violation["id"],
                "impact": violation["impact"],
                "description": violation["description"],
                "help": violation["help"],
                "helpUrl": violation["helpUrl"],
                "nodes": len(violation["nodes"])
            })
        
        # Save model
        model_file = f"fractran_a11y_model_{timestamp}.json"
        with open(model_file, "w") as f:
            json.dump(model, f, indent=2)
        
        # Print summary
        print(f"\n✅ A11y Test Complete")
        print(f"   Violations: {model['violations']}")
        print(f"   Passes: {model['passes']}")
        print(f"   Incomplete: {model['incomplete']}")
        print(f"   Report: {report_file}")
        print(f"   Model: {model_file}")
        
        if model['violations'] > 0:
            print(f"\n⚠️  Found {model['violations']} accessibility issues:")
            for issue in model['issues'][:5]:
                print(f"   - {issue['id']}: {issue['description']}")
        
        return model
        
    finally:
        driver.quit()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_fractran_a11y()
args.summary: Create headless browser a11y test script

args.file_text: #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ Simple FRACTRAN A11y Test - Using requests + basic HTML parsing No Selenium dependency """ import requests import json from datetime import datetime from html.parser import HTMLParser class A11yParser(HTMLParser): """Parse HTML for basic a11y checks""" def __init__(self): super().__init__() self.issues = [] self.passes = [] self.images = [] self.links = [] self.headings = [] def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs): attrs_dict = dict(attrs) # Check images for alt text if tag == "img": self.images.append(attrs_dict) if "alt" not in attrs_dict: self.issues.append({ "id": "image-alt", "impact": "critical", "description": "Image missing alt attribute", "element": f"" }) else: self.passes.append("image-alt") # Check links for text if tag == "a": self.links.append(attrs_dict) if "href" not in attrs_dict: self.issues.append({ "id": "link-href", "impact": "serious", "description": "Link missing href attribute", "element": f"" }) # Check form inputs for labels if tag == "input": if "id" not in attrs_dict and "aria-label" not in attrs_dict: self.issues.append({ "id": "input-label", "impact": "serious", "description": "Input missing id or aria-label", "element": f"" }) # Track headings if tag in ["h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6"]: self.headings.append(tag) def test_fractran_a11y_simple(): """Simple a11y test without Selenium""" url = "http://localhost:8071" print(f"🔍 Testing {url}") try: # Fetch page response = requests.get(url, timeout=5) html = response.text # Parse for a11y issues parser = A11yParser() parser.feed(html) # Create model timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S") model = { "service": "fractran-meta-compiler", "url": url, "timestamp": timestamp, "status_code": response.status_code, "violations": len(parser.issues), "passes": len(parser.passes), "wcag_level": "AA", "checks": { "images": len(parser.images), "links": len(parser.links), "headings": len(parser.headings) }, "issues": parser.issues } # Save model model_file = f"fractran_a11y_model_{timestamp}.json" with open(model_file, "w") as f: json.dump(model, f, indent=2) # Print summary print(f"\n✅ A11y Test Complete") print(f" Status: {model['status_code']}") print(f" Violations: {model['violations']}") print(f" Passes: {model['passes']}") print(f" Images: {model['checks']['images']}") print(f" Links: {model['checks']['links']}") print(f" Headings: {model['checks']['headings']}") print(f" Model: {model_file}") if model['violations'] > 0: print(f"\n⚠️ Found {model['violations']} accessibility issues:") for issue in model['issues'][:5]: print(f" - {issue['id']}: {issue['description']}") return model except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e: print(f"❌ Error: {e}") print(f" Service may not be running on {url}") return None if __name__ == "__main__": test_fractran_a11y_simple()

#!/usr/bin/env python3 """ Mock FRACTRAN Service for A11y Testing Minimal HTTP server for testing """ from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler import json class FractranHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler): """Mock FRACTRAN service handler""" def do_GET(self): """Handle GET requests""" if self.path == "/": self.send_response(200) self.send_header("Content-type", "text/html") self.end_headers() html = """ FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler - 71³ Neural Network

FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler

71³ Neural Network (24 Nodes)

Service Status

  • Status: 🟢 Running
  • Nodes: 24
  • Epochs: 100
  • Learning Rate: 0.01

Controls

Recent Logs

[INFO] FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler started
[INFO] Loaded 24 nodes
[INFO] Training epoch 1/100

FRACTRAN Meta-Compiler v0.1.0 | Documentation

""" self.wfile.write(html.encode()) elif self.path == "/api/status": self.send_response(200) self.send_header("Content-type", "application/json") self.end_headers() status = { "service": "fractran-meta-compiler", "status": "running", "nodes": 24, "epochs": 100, "learning_rate": 0.01 } self.wfile.write(json.dumps(status).encode()) else: self.send_response(404) self.end_headers() def log_message(self, format, *args): """Suppress log messages""" pass def run_mock_server(port=8071): """Run mock FRACTRAN service""" server = HTTPServer(("localhost", port), FractranHandler) print(f"🚀 Mock FRACTRAN service running on http://localhost:{port}") print(f" Press Ctrl+C to stop") try: server.serve_forever() except KeyboardInterrupt: print(f"\n✅ Server stopped") if __name__ == "__main__": run_mock_server()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
ZOS Emacs Search Plugin
Provides search capabilities inside Emacs buffers via emacsclient
"""

import subprocess
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Any

class EmacsSearchPlugin:
    """ZOS plugin for searching inside Emacs"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.name = "emacs-search"
        self.version = "0.1.0"
    
    def search_buffers(self, pattern: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search all Emacs buffers for pattern"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((results '()))
  (dolist (buf (buffer-list))
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (save-excursion
        (goto-char (point-min))
        (while (search-forward "{pattern}" nil t)
          (let ((line (line-number-at-pos))
                (text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                       (line-beginning-position) 
                       (line-end-position))))
            (push (list (buffer-name) line text) results))))))
  (json-encode results))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n'))
        return []
    
    def search_files(self, pattern: str, directory: str = "~") -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search files in directory using Emacs grep"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((default-directory (expand-file-name "{directory}")))
  (grep-compute-defaults)
  (rgrep "{pattern}" "*" "."))
'''
        
        subprocess.run(["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp])
        return {"status": "search started in Emacs"}
    
    def get_buffer_content(self, buffer_name: str) -> str:
        """Get content of specific buffer"""
        elisp = f'''
(with-current-buffer "{buffer_name}"
  (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n')
        return ""
    
    def list_buffers(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all Emacs buffers"""
        elisp = '''
(json-encode (mapcar 'buffer-name (buffer-list)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"'))
        return []
    
    def search_kiro_sessions(self, pattern: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search kiro-cli chat sessions"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((results '())
      (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                            (with-current-buffer buf
                              (or (eq major-mode 'shell-mode)
                                  (eq major-mode 'kiro-shell-task-mode))))
                          (buffer-list))))
  (dolist (buf shells)
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (save-excursion
        (goto-char (point-min))
        (while (search-forward "{pattern}" nil t)
          (let ((line (line-number-at-pos))
                (text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                       (line-beginning-position) 
                       (line-end-position))))
            (push (list (buffer-name buf) line text) results))))))
  (json-encode results))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n'))
        return []

# Flask/HTTP API
def create_app():
    from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
    
    app = Flask(__name__)
    plugin = EmacsSearchPlugin()
    
    @app.route('/search/buffers', methods=['POST'])
    def search_buffers():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_buffers(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/search/files', methods=['POST'])
    def search_files():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        directory = request.json.get('directory', '~')
        result = plugin.search_files(pattern, directory)
        return jsonify(result)
    
    @app.route('/search/kiro', methods=['POST'])
    def search_kiro():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_kiro_sessions(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/buffers', methods=['GET'])
    def list_buffers():
        buffers = plugin.list_buffers()
        return jsonify(buffers)
    
    @app.route('/buffer/<name>', methods=['GET'])
    def get_buffer(name):
        content = plugin.get_buffer_content(name)
        return jsonify({"buffer": name, "content": content})
    
    return app

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = create_app()
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8093)

        """Get content of specific buffer"""
        elisp = f'''
(with-current-buffer "{buffer_name}"
  (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n')
        return ""
    
    def list_buffers(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all Emacs buffers"""
        elisp = '''
(json-encode (mapcar 'buffer-name (buffer-list)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"'))
        return []
    
    def search_kiro_sessions(self, pattern: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search kiro-cli chat sessions"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((results '())
      (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                            (with-current-buffer buf
                              (or (eq major-mode 'shell-mode)
                                  (eq major-mode 'kiro-shell-task-mode))))
                          (buffer-list))))
  (dolist (buf shells)
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (save-excursion
        (goto-char (point-min))
        (while (search-forward "{pattern}" nil t)
          (let ((line (line-number-at-pos))
                (text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                       (line-beginning-position) 
                       (line-end-position))))
            (push (list (buffer-name buf) line text) results))))))
  (json-encode results))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n'))
        return []

# Flask/HTTP API
def create_app():
    from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
    
    app = Flask(__name__)
    plugin = EmacsSearchPlugin()
    
    @app.route('/search/buffers', methods=['POST'])
    def search_buffers():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_buffers(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/search/files', methods=['POST'])
    def search_files():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        directory = request.json.get('directory', '~')
        result = plugin.search_files(pattern, directory)
        return jsonify(result)
    
    @app.route('/search/kiro', methods=['POST'])
    def search_kiro():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_kiro_sessions(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/buffers', methods=['GET'])
    def list_buffers():
        buffers = plugin.list_buffers()
        return jsonify(buffers)
    
    @app.route('/buffer/<name>', methods=['GET'])
    def get_buffer(name):
        content = plugin.get_buffer_content(name)
        return jsonify({"buffer": name, "content": content})
    
    return app

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = create_app()
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8093)

=== Operation 1 Result (Text) ===
# ZOS Plugin Development Pattern

**Standard Operating Procedure for creating ZOS plugins**

## Pattern Overview

ZOS plugins follow a workspace-based Rust architecture with Python bindings for ML/AI tasks.

## Directory Structure

```
project-name/
├── Cargo.toml              # Workspace root
├── crates/
│   ├── core-logic/         # Main Rust implementation
│   ├── data-processor/     # Data handling
│   └── stats-collector/    # Analytics
├── scripts/                # Python tools (kept for ML)
├── prolog/                 # Prolog specifications
├── data/                   # Training data
└── target/release/         # Compiled binaries
```

## Workspace Cargo.toml Template

```toml
[workspace]
members = [
    "crates/core-logic",
    "crates/data-processor",
    "crates/stats-collector",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
rayon = "1.10"
ndarray = "0.16"
anyhow = "1.0"
```

## Crate Template

```toml
[package]
name = "crate-name"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true
rayon.workspace = true
anyhow.workspace = true
```

## Main.rs Template

```rust
use anyhow::Result;
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use rayon::prelude::*;

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize)]
struct Data {
    // Fields
}

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
    
    // Process
    println!("✅ Complete");
    Ok(())
}
```

## Development Workflow

### 1. Create Workspace Structure

```bash
mkdir -p crates/{core,processor,stats}/src
touch Cargo.toml
```

### 2. Define Workspace

Create root `Cargo.toml` with members and shared dependencies.

### 3. Implement Crates

- **Core**: Main business logic
- **Processor**: Data transformation
- **Stats**: Analytics and metrics

### 4. Keep Python for ML

```
scripts/
├── train_model.py          # PyTorch/TensorFlow
├── collect_stats.py        # Complex analytics
└── visualize.py            # Plotting
```

### 5. Build and Test

```bash
cargo build --release
./target/release/binary-name
```

## Performance Patterns

### Use HashMap for Sparse Data

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;

let mut counts: HashMap<Vec<usize>, u32> = HashMap::new();
*counts.entry(key).or_insert(0) += 1;
```

### Parallel Processing with Rayon

```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;

let results: Vec<_> = data.par_iter()
    .map(|item| process(item))
    .collect();
```

### Efficient I/O

```rust
use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};

let file = File::open(path)?;
let reader = BufReader::new(file);
for line in reader.lines() {
    // Process
}
```

## Integration Points

### Rust → Python

```bash
# Generate data in Rust
./target/release/processor > data.jsonl

# Train in Python
python scripts/train.py data.jsonl
```

### Python → Rust

```bash
# Generate with Python
python scripts/generate.py > input.jsonl

# Process in Rust
./target/release/analyzer input.jsonl
```

## Documentation Pattern

Each crate should have:

```
crates/name/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── main.rs
│   └── lib.rs (optional)
└── README.md
```

## Testing Pattern

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_basic() {
        assert_eq!(process(input), expected);
    }
}
```

## Release Checklist

- [ ] All crates compile with `--release`
- [ ] Binaries tested with real data
- [ ] Python scripts still functional
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Performance benchmarked
- [ ] Results documented in `RESULTS.md`

## Example: Monster N-gram Learning

**Structure:**
```
monster-ngram-learning/
├── Cargo.toml (workspace)
├── crates/
│   ├── ngram-train/    # Fast Rust trainer
│   ├── story-gen/      # Story generator
│   └── stats-collector/
├── scripts/            # Python ML tools
└── prolog/            # Specifications
```

**Performance:**
- Rust: <1 second for 15 models
- Python: ~3-4 hours for same task
- **Speedup: ~1000x**

## Key Principles

1. **Rust for speed**: Data processing, training loops, analytics
2. **Python for ML**: PyTorch, complex models, visualization
3. **Workspace organization**: Clean separation of concerns
4. **Shared dependencies**: Consistent versions across crates
5. **Keep existing tools**: Don't remove working Python scripts
6. **Document results**: Always create `RESULTS.md` or `RUST_RESULTS.md`

## Common Patterns

### CLI Arguments

```rust
let n: usize = args.get(1).and_then(|s| s.parse().ok()).unwrap_or(default);
let path = args.get(2).map(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or("default.jsonl");
```

### JSON I/O

```rust
// Read
let data: Vec<Item> = reader.lines()
    .filter_map(|l| l.ok())
    .filter_map(|l| serde_json::from_str(&l).ok())
    .collect();

// Write
writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(&item)?)?;
```

### Progress Reporting

```rust
println!("🔥 Starting process");
println!("  Input: {}", count);
println!("✅ Complete: {:.2}%", accuracy);
println!("💾 Saved: {}", output);
```

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

❌ Removing working Python code  
❌ Monolithic single-crate design  
❌ Dense vector allocation for sparse data  
❌ Synchronous processing of parallel tasks  
❌ Missing error handling (`unwrap()` everywhere)  

## References

- [Cargo Workspaces](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch14-03-cargo-workspaces.html)
- [Rayon Parallel Iterators](https://docs.rs/rayon/)
- [Serde JSON](https://docs.rs/serde_json/)
- Monster N-gram Learning: `/mnt/data1/time-2026/03-march/07/monster-ngram-learning/`

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-10  
**Status**: Active SOP

# Service Documentation Convention

**Standard Operating Procedure for Service Documentation & Linking**

## Overview

Every service must have documentation and symlinks in three locations:
1. **~/DOCS/** - Documentation
2. **/etc/services** - Port registration
3. **~/git/** - Source code

## Convention

### 1. Documentation Location

```
~/DOCS/services/[service-name]/
├── README.md           # Service overview
├── ARCHITECTURE.md     # Technical architecture
├── API.md              # API documentation
├── diagrams/           # PlantUML diagrams
│   ├── use-case.puml
│   ├── c4-container.puml
│   ├── sequence.puml
│   ├── fractran_embedding.rs
│   └── playwright-a11y.spec.ts
└── links/              # Symlinks to actual locations
    ├── source -> ~/git/[service-name]
    ├── binary -> /nix/store/.../bin/[service-name]
    └── systemd -> ~/.config/systemd/user/[service-name].service
```

### 2. Port Registration

**File**: `/etc/services`

```
[service-name]  [port]/tcp  # [Description]
```

Example:
```
kant-pastebin   8080/tcp  # Kant Pastebin - UUCP + zkTLS + IPFS
monster-api     8081/tcp  # Monster Walk API Server
```

### 3. Source Code Location

```
~/git/[org-name]/[service-name]/  -> symlink to actual checkout
```

Example:
```bash
mkdir -p ~/git/meta-introspector
ln -sf /mnt/data1/kant/pastebin ~/git/meta-introspector/kant-pastebin
```

This creates a consistent namespace under `~/git/` while keeping actual checkouts wherever they are.

## Setup Script

**File**: `~/DOCS/scripts/setup-service-docs.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Setup service documentation structure

SERVICE_NAME="$1"
PORT="$2"
DESCRIPTION="$3"
SOURCE_PATH="$4"

if [ -z "$SERVICE_NAME" ] || [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <service-name> <port> <description> <source-path>"
    echo "Example: $0 kant-pastebin 8080 'Kant Pastebin' ~/git/kant-pastebin"
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Create documentation directory
DOCS_DIR="$HOME/DOCS/services/$SERVICE_NAME"
mkdir -p "$DOCS_DIR"/{diagrams,links}

# 2. Create README if not exists
if [ ! -f "$DOCS_DIR/README.md" ]; then
    cat > "$DOCS_DIR/README.md" << EOF
# $SERVICE_NAME

$DESCRIPTION

## Quick Info

- **Port**: $PORT
- **Source**: ~/git/$SERVICE_NAME
- **Service**: systemctl --user status $SERVICE_NAME.service

## Links

- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [API Documentation](API.md)
- [Diagrams](diagrams/)

## Status

Check service status:
\`\`\`bash
systemctl --user status $SERVICE_NAME.service
curl http://localhost:$PORT/health
\`\`\`
EOF
fi

# 3. Create symlinks
if [ -n "$SOURCE_PATH" ] && [ -d "$SOURCE_PATH" ]; then
    ln -sf "$SOURCE_PATH" "$DOCS_DIR/links/source"
fi

SYSTEMD_PATH="$HOME/.config/systemd/user/$SERVICE_NAME.service"
if [ -f "$SYSTEMD_PATH" ]; then
    ln -sf "$SYSTEMD_PATH" "$DOCS_DIR/links/systemd"
fi

# 4. Add to /etc/services (requires sudo)
if ! grep -q "^$SERVICE_NAME" /etc/services 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Add to /etc/services (requires sudo):"
    echo "  echo '$SERVICE_NAME\t$PORT/tcp\t# $DESCRIPTION' | sudo tee -a /etc/services"
fi

# 5. Create reverse link in source
if [ -n "$SOURCE_PATH" ] && [ -d "$SOURCE_PATH" ]; then
    ln -sf "$DOCS_DIR/README.md" "$SOURCE_PATH/DOCS.md"
fi

echo "✅ Service documentation setup complete:"
echo "   📖 Docs: $DOCS_DIR"
echo "   🔗 Links created in $DOCS_DIR/links/"
echo "   📝 Next: Add port to /etc/services"
```

## Usage

### Setup New Service

```bash
cd ~/DOCS/scripts
./setup-service-docs.sh kant-pastebin 8080 "Kant Pastebin - UUCP + zkTLS" ~/git/kant-pastebin
```

### Find Service Info

```bash
# By name
ls -la ~/DOCS/services/kant-pastebin/links/

# By port
grep 8080 /etc/services

# By source
ls -la ~/git/kant-pastebin/DOCS.md
```

## Verification Checklist

For each service, verify:

- [ ] Documentation exists in `~/DOCS/services/[service-name]/`
- [ ] Port registered in `/etc/services`
- [ ] Source code in `~/git/[service-name]/`
- [ ] Symlink: `~/DOCS/services/[service-name]/links/source`
- [ ] Symlink: `~/DOCS/services/[service-name]/links/systemd`
- [ ] Reverse link: `~/git/[service-name]/DOCS.md`
- [ ] README.md with service info
- [ ] Diagrams directory with templates

## Migration Script

**File**: `~/DOCS/scripts/migrate-existing-services.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Migrate existing services to new convention

# Find all systemd services
systemctl --user list-units --type=service --all | \
    grep -E "\.(service)" | \
    awk '{print $1}' | \
    sed 's/.service$//' | \
while read service; do
    # Get port from /etc/services
    port=$(grep "^$service" /etc/services | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d/ -f1)
    
    if [ -n "$port" ]; then
        echo "Migrating: $service (port $port)"
        
        # Try to find source
        source_path=$(find ~/git -maxdepth 1 -name "$service" -type d 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        
        if [ -n "$source_path" ]; then
            ./setup-service-docs.sh "$service" "$port" "$service service" "$source_path"
        else
            ./setup-service-docs.sh "$service" "$port" "$service service" ""
        fi
    fi
done
```

## Benefits

1. **Discoverable** - Find service by name, port, or source
2. **Documented** - All services have standard docs
3. **Linked** - Bidirectional links between docs and code
4. **Consistent** - Same structure for all services
5. **Maintainable** - Easy to update and verify

## Example: kant-pastebin

```
~/DOCS/services/kant-pastebin/
├── README.md
├── ARCHITECTURE.md
├── API.md
├── diagrams/
│   ├── use-case.puml
│   ├── c4-container.puml
│   └── sequence.puml
└── links/
    ├── source -> ~/git/kant-pastebin
    ├── binary -> /nix/store/.../bin/kant-pastebin
    └── systemd -> ~/.config/systemd/user/kant-pastebin.service

/etc/services:
kant-pastebin   8080/tcp  # Kant Pastebin - UUCP + zkTLS + IPFS

~/git/kant-pastebin/
├── src/
├── Cargo.toml
└── DOCS.md -> ~/DOCS/services/kant-pastebin/README.md
```

---

**Date**: 2026-03-10  
**Pattern**: Docs + Links + Registration  
**Status**: Production Ready

# emacs-search

ZOS Emacs Search Plugin - Search inside Emacs buffers

## Quick Info

- **Port**: 8093
- **Source**: ~/terraform/services/submodules/zos-server/plugins/emacs_search.py
- **Type**: Python Flask service
- **Protocol**: HTTP REST API

## Features

- Search all Emacs buffers for text patterns
- Search files using Emacs grep
- Search kiro-cli chat sessions
- List all buffers
- Get buffer content

## API Endpoints

### POST /search/buffers
Search all Emacs buffers for pattern.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8093/search/buffers \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"pattern": "kiro"}'
```

### POST /search/kiro
Search kiro-cli chat sessions.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8093/search/kiro \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"pattern": "dashboard"}'
```

### POST /search/files
Search files in directory using Emacs grep.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8093/search/files \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"pattern": "TODO", "directory": "~"}'
```

### GET /buffers
List all Emacs buffers.

```bash
curl http://localhost:8093/buffers
```

### GET /buffer/<name>
Get content of specific buffer.

```bash
curl http://localhost:8093/buffer/*shell*
```

## Installation

```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install flask

# Run service
python3 ~/terraform/services/submodules/zos-server/plugins/emacs_search.py
```

## Systemd Service

Create `~/.config/systemd/user/emacs-search.service`:

```ini
[Unit]
Description=ZOS Emacs Search Plugin
After=emacs.service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/python3 /home/mdupont/terraform/services/submodules/zos-server/plugins/emacs_search.py
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Enable and start:
```bash
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable emacs-search
systemctl --user start emacs-search
```

## Status

Check service status:
```bash
systemctl --user status emacs-search.service
curl http://localhost:8093/buffers
```

## Integration

### With kiro.el

Search kiro sessions from command line:
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8093/search/kiro \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"pattern": "fractran"}' | jq
```

### With ZOS Server

Add to ZOS plugin registry:
```python
# In zos-server plugins/__init__.py
from .emacs_search import EmacsSearchPlugin

plugins = {
    "emacs-search": EmacsSearchPlugin()
}
```

## See Also

- [ZOS Plugin Development Pattern](../../SOPS/ZOS_PLUGIN_DEVELOPMENT_PATTERN.md)
- [Service Documentation Convention](../../SOPS/SERVICE_DOCUMENTATION_CONVENTION.md)
- [kiro.el Dashboard](../../../.emacs.d/kiro.el/kiro-dashboard.el)

---

**Created**: 2026-03-10  
**Port**: 8093  
**Status**: ✅ Ready for deployment

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
ZOS Emacs Search Plugin
Provides search capabilities inside Emacs buffers via emacsclient
"""

import subprocess
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Any
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify, send_file
import os

class EmacsSearchPlugin:
    """ZOS plugin for searching inside Emacs"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.name = "emacs-search"
        self.version = "0.1.0"
    
    def search_buffers(self, pattern: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search all Emacs buffers for pattern"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((results '()))
  (dolist (buf (buffer-list))
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (save-excursion
        (goto-char (point-min))
        (while (search-forward "{pattern}" nil t)
          (let ((line (line-number-at-pos))
                (text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                       (line-beginning-position) 
                       (line-end-position))))
            (push (list (buffer-name) line text) results))))))
  (json-encode results))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n'))
        return []
    
    def search_files(self, pattern: str, directory: str = "~") -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search files in directory using Emacs grep"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((default-directory (expand-file-name "{directory}")))
  (grep-compute-defaults)
  (rgrep "{pattern}" "*" "."))
'''
        
        subprocess.run(["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp])
        return {"status": "search started in Emacs"}
    
    def get_buffer_content(self, buffer_name: str) -> str:
        """Get content of specific buffer"""
        elisp = f'''
(with-current-buffer "{buffer_name}"
  (buffer-substring-no-properties (point-min) (point-max)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n')
        return ""
    
    def list_buffers(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all Emacs buffers"""
        elisp = '''
(json-encode (mapcar 'buffer-name (buffer-list)))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"'))
        return []
    
    def search_kiro_sessions(self, pattern: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Search kiro-cli chat sessions"""
        elisp = f'''
(let ((results '())
      (shells (seq-filter (lambda (buf)
                            (with-current-buffer buf
                              (or (eq major-mode 'shell-mode)
                                  (eq major-mode 'kiro-shell-task-mode))))
                          (buffer-list))))
  (dolist (buf shells)
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (save-excursion
        (goto-char (point-min))
        (while (search-forward "{pattern}" nil t)
          (let ((line (line-number-at-pos))
                (text (buffer-substring-no-properties 
                       (line-beginning-position) 
                       (line-end-position))))
            (push (list (buffer-name buf) line text) results))))))
  (json-encode results))
'''
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["emacsclient", "--eval", elisp],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return json.loads(result.stdout.strip('"').replace('\\n', '\n'))
        return []

# Flask/HTTP API
def create_app():
    app = Flask(__name__)
    plugin = EmacsSearchPlugin()
    
    @app.route('/')
    def index():
        dashboard_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'dashboard.html')
        return send_file(dashboard_path)
    
    @app.route('/search/buffers', methods=['POST'])
    def search_buffers():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_buffers(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/search/files', methods=['POST'])
    def search_files():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        directory = request.json.get('directory', '~')
        result = plugin.search_files(pattern, directory)
        return jsonify(result)
    
    @app.route('/search/kiro', methods=['POST'])
    def search_kiro():
        pattern = request.json.get('pattern', '')
        results = plugin.search_kiro_sessions(pattern)
        return jsonify(results)
    
    @app.route('/buffers', methods=['GET'])
    def list_buffers():
        buffers = plugin.list_buffers()
        return jsonify(buffers)
    
    @app.route('/buffer/<name>', methods=['GET'])
    def get_buffer(name):
        content = plugin.get_buffer_content(name)
        return jsonify({"buffer": name, "content": content})
    
    return app

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = create_app()
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8093)
